Create a SqlRender SQL File

Source: R/make.R
makeSrcSqlFile.Rd

Creates a new SQL script in the analysis/src/sql folder for storing parameterized OMOP queries. These files are rendered using SqlRender and executed against the CDM.

Usage

makeSrcSqlFile(
  fileName,
  author = NULL,
  description = NULL,
  projectPath = here::here(),
  openFile = TRUE
)

Arguments

fileName

The name of the SQL file (e.g., "condition_occurrence_query")

author

The name of the author. Defaults to template text if NULL

description

A brief description of what this query does. Defaults to template text if NULL

projectPath

The path to the project (defaults to current project)

openFile

Whether to open the file after creating it (default TRUE)

Value

Invisible character string containing the template content

Details

SQL files in src/sql are parameterized queries meant to be rendered with SqlRender::render() before execution. They should use @ notation for parameters (e.g., @cdmDatabaseSchema). Document all parameters used in your query with comments at the top of the file.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Create a SQL query for analyzing condition_occurrence
makeSrcSqlFile(
  fileName = "condition_occurrence_counts",
  author = "Jane Doe",
  description = "Count conditions by month in the CDM"
)

# Create with minimal arguments
makeSrcSqlFile(fileName = "get_person_demographics")
} # }
